branching coral.importance: Provides shelter for juvenile fish and invertebrates.
branching coral.health_indicator: Highly sensitive to bleaching and physical damage.

staghorn coral.importance: One of the fastest-growing reef-building corals.

elkhorn coral.importance: Protects coastlines by reducing wave energy.

brain coral.importance: Long-lived coral providing stable reef structure.

table coral.importance: Provides shade and habitat for reef organisms.

plate coral.importance: Efficient at capturing sunlight in deeper waters.

mushroom coral.importance: Usually free-living and found on sandy reef bottoms.

bleached coral.importance: Represents coral under severe environmental stress.
